title: Laptop Battery Health
description: Generate battery evidence, inspect power settings, and summarize battery wear, runtime risk, and next steps.
starter_prompt: Check laptop battery health and explain any wear or runtime risk.

phases.prompt: Scope battery-health evidence and note that battery report generation writes a diagnostic artifact only.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; $out=Join-Path $env:TEMP 'sak-battery-report.html'; powercfg /batteryreport /output $out | Out-String | Write-Output; Write-Output ('Battery report: ' + $out); Get-CimInstance Win32_Battery -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object Name,Status,BatteryStatus,EstimatedChargeRemaining,EstimatedRunTime | Format-List; powercfg /getactivescheme | Out-String | Write-Output

phases.prompt: Generate battery report artifact and collect battery/power-plan evidence. Do not change settings.
phases.completion: Battery report path and battery evidence are captured.

phases.prompt: Analyze battery evidence. Summarize wear/runtime concerns, missing data, whether the device appears to have a battery, and next steps.
Check that findings distinguish generated report artifact, live battery status, and unsupported desktop/no-battery cases.

Create a technician and customer-ready handoff report with evidence, findings, actions/tools used, verification status, artifacts/logs, cleanup status, residual risk, and prioritized follow-up next actions.
Keep battery report path, logs, and reports. Remove only workflow-created scratch files if user asks.

acceptance_criteria: Battery report artifact path is captured when supported.
Battery and active power-plan evidence are reviewed.
Final report includes evidence, actions, cleanup status, risks, and follow-up items.
